steps.title: Read the y-intercept
steps.content: The line crosses the y-axis at $y = 12$. This is the y-intercept, and all four choices have it, so focus on the slope.

steps.title: Find the slope using two points
steps.content:
Select two clear points on the line:
  • $(0, 12)$ where the line crosses the y-axis
  • $(6, 6)$ at the intersection grid point
Slope: $$m = \frac{6 - 12}{6 - 0} = \frac{-6}{6} = -1$$

steps.title: Write the equation and match
steps.content: Using slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$ with $m = -1$ and $b = 12$: $$y = -x + 12$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
